title: Congee with Century Egg

seo.title: How to Make Congee with Century Egg — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Congee with Century Egg. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas for a comforting Chinese breakfast.
longTailKeywords: how to make Congee with Century Egg
easy Congee with Century Egg recipe
authentic Chinese Congee with Century Egg
lowCompetitionKeywords: Chinese Congee with Century Egg recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Congee with Century Egg Recipe
h2Headings: What is Congee with Century Egg?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Congee with Century Egg recipe garnished with green onions and ginger
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Congee with Century Egg — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Experience the comforting warmth of Congee with Century Egg, a beloved Chinese breakfast staple. This creamy rice porridge, enriched with the unique flavor of century eggs and often tender pork, offers a savory and deeply satisfying start to your day. It is a nourishing dish that brings a taste of traditional Chinese home cooking to your table.

ingredients.item: Lean pork (e.g., pork loin or tenderloin)

instructions.title: Prepare the Rice
instructions.text: Wash the long-grain white rice thoroughly until the water runs clear. For a creamier congee, you can soak the rice in water for 30 minutes to an hour before cooking, then drain.

instructions.title: Start Cooking the Congee Base
instructions.text: In a large pot, combine the washed rice and 8-10 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil over high heat, stirring occasionally to prevent the rice from sticking to the bottom. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot partially, and let it simmer.

instructions.title: Prepare Other Ingredients
instructions.text: While the congee simmers, prepare your century eggs by peeling and dicing them into small pieces. Slice the lean pork thinly or mince it. Peel and julienne half of the ginger, and finely mince the other half. Chop the green onions for garnish.

instructions.title: Add Pork and Ginger
instructions.text: After the congee has simmered for about 20-25 minutes and the rice grains have started to break down, add the sliced or minced pork and the finely minced ginger to the pot. Stir well to combine and ensure the pork cooks evenly. Continue to simmer for another 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Incorporate Century Eggs and Season
instructions.text: Add the diced century eggs to the congee. Stir gently and let it simmer for another 3-5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld. Season the congee generously with salt and white pepper to your preference. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Congee with Century Egg into individual serving bowls. Garnish each bowl with the julienned fresh ginger and chopped green onions. For an extra touch of aroma, drizzle a small amount of sesame oil over each serving before enjoying.

tips: For an extra creamy congee, freeze the washed rice for a few hours or overnight before cooking. The freezing process breaks down the rice grains, leading to a much smoother texture.
When selecting century eggs, look for ones with firm, clear jelly-like whites and creamy yolks. The aroma should be pungent but not overwhelmingly sulfuric. Store them in a cool, dry place.

Congee is best enjoyed fresh. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water if it has thickened too much.
article: Congee with Century Egg is a beloved Chinese Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
